subject: Providence
body_html:
Hi Jeffery
 

I hope you're well. I lost touch with you again! bummer! Anyway let me know what you think of this- 
My friend is currently looking for investors in an energy project. He has an oil exploration and production company,  Providence Energy Group (www.providenceenergygroup.com), with operations in Houston and Central America. They are working in Belize (where commercial quantities of oil has been discovered), with a 25 year contract for 340,000 acres of mineral concessions. Providence is currently in the process of acquiring several oil properties, including a producing oil property south of Houston, which has revenues, production and reserves. The acquisition is very low risk (since it is currently producing cash), and has a high return, especially with current oil prices. The field will repay financing in less than 18 months, through cash flow, and has an upside of about $50 million+ over the next four to five years. The investor will receive 75% of the project.  In addition to these fields, Providence has other investment opportunities including a field in California, which will cost $7 million (for 40% working interest in the project) which could pay out about $500 million in the next 8 to 10 years (agian, the investor will recieve 75%). I think they would be a good company to get to know.
 
I wouldn't pass on an investment prospect if I didn't think is was sound. Please contact me if you may have interest in working with Providence.
 
If you have interest, I will pass on the contact information to you.
 
Oh and it would have been great to see you too!
 
Thanks!
 
Nanthida
